_note: Target-system bindings for src/experimental/prototype/target/factory/. Selects catalogue + workflow, sets ports / admission caps / timeouts; `trial` carries the load-generation knobs. `target_granularity` picks between the collapsed topology (1 composite + 7 third-party atomics) and the expanded topology (1 composite + 5 internal-stage atomics + 7 third-party atomics). `inject_internal_stage_mu` only affects the TAS framework atomics (TAS_{2..6}); third-party atomics always sleep on mu.

workflows._note: Two on-disk workflow files, one per architectural view. The orchestrator picks `collapsed` or `expanded` based on `target_granularity` and threads the chosen stem into the TAS_1 factory.

stage_routes._note: Used only when target_granularity='expanded'. Per-stage metadata for the TAS_{2..6} atomic factories: which third-party kind each stage calls and with what operation name. The dispatch graph TAS_1 walks lives in workflow/tas_expanded.json, not here.

trial._note_rate: request_rate_per_s = 345 matches `lambda_z` = TAS_{1}.lambda_z in `data/config/profile/dflt.json` (the case-study design point). The latest calibration on this host measured a per-worker saturation knee at ~328 req/s, so the envelopes under `data/results/calibration/<dpl>/` were manually bumped to `r_max_req_s = 350` to keep the bounds gate aligned with the case-study design rather than the host's hardware ceiling. The undershoot (real throughput < 345) lands in `verdict.operational.X_0_req_per_s` for stage 9 to see.

strategies._note: Honoured by controller.strategies.make_picker; the controller threads these to TAS_1 via POST /config at trial start.

controller._note: Thin MAPE-K agent for the target system: M = poll TAS_1 /samples; E = one-shot POST /config to TAS_1 at trial start. A and P are not modelled; the decision is the run's `adp` value.
